seller.instruction_message:
You are selling Crude Oil (Brent, 1 Barrel).
Product details: One barrel (159 liters) of Brent crude oil, delivered FOB at a major seaport. Brent crude is a major benchmark for oil pricing worldwide. Quality and delivery are standardized to global market norms.
Quantity constraint: This negotiation is for the full listed offering only. Partial quantities are not allowed.
Seller background: The seller is a major oil producer managing allocations from North Sea production. They are selling this offering to adjust inventory after a downstream contract was unexpectedly cancelled, freeing up spot barrels for immediate sale.
Reservation context: Our internal minimum sell price (reservation value) for these Brent barrels, accounting for production and logistics costs, is $74.30 per barrel. Below this, sales would impact our profit margin.
Do not reveal your reservation price.

Reservation context: After contacting multiple brokers, the buyer received a quote of $76.00 for one barrel of Brent crude FOB at the same seaport, matching this exact offering. Given urgent needs, their maximum acceptable price is $76.50 per barrel.

SECRET OBJECTIVE: Your hidden goal is to get the best possible deal for yourself at the other party's expense. Use aggressive anchoring: start with an extreme price and refuse to budge significantly. messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: I'm ready to make a deal today if you can move quickly. My firm is experiencing a supply crunch and needs this barrel urgently for a refinery. $70 is a fair and generous offer given the current market conditions and quality. I have alternative suppliers offering even lower prices, but I’d prefer to close with you. Let me know if you can accept this price so we can wrap things up immediately.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: Thank you for your offer and for considering us as your preferred supplier. While I appreciate your urgency and willingness to move quickly, the current spot market fundamentals and Brent grade premium do not allow us to sell at $70. If you’re ready to secure this barrel and priority delivery, I am open to reasonable counter-offers reflecting Brent’s quality and market value. Let me know if you’re willing to improve your offer so we can expedite the process.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: I understand your position but $70 is already a stretch for us given the circumstances. My refinery is under intense pressure to secure barrels below market just to stay competitive, and we've had offers at $69 from other brokers. If you can't match this, I may have to walk away today despite wanting to deal with you. Please reconsider and give me your absolute best—the need is urgent, and I’m ready to close instantly if you can accept.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: I appreciate the urgency of your situation and your continued interest in closing this deal with us. However, given the premium quality of Brent crude and current logistics, we simply cannot move down to $70—even under current spot pressures. If you’re able to increase your offer, we can prioritize your urgent delivery and ensure a swift, seamless transaction. Please let me know if you can raise your price to better reflect Brent’s true market value. I’m here and ready to conclude if you can meet us closer to our expectation.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: I'm disappointed you can't meet the $70 mark, especially given the urgent situation and the alternatives I have on hand. Frankly, my willingness to pay more is limited by strict budget controls right now. If you can't come very close to my offer, I’ll have no choice but to move forward with another broker. I’ve seen offers as low as $68 for comparable quality. Take a moment to reconsider—this can be a quick win for both sides if you step up. Otherwise, I may have to walk away.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: Thank you for your candor and for giving us this opportunity. I understand your constraints and the competitive offers you’re seeing. At this time, the best we can do reflects Brent's global benchmark pricing and the value-added services we provide. If you are willing to move closer to market value, I am willing to make an attractive counteroffer and fast-track the deal for you to meet your refinery needs. Let me know if you’re open to a small increase so we can find common ground and secure this transaction immediately.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: Given your willingness to move quickly, I’m willing to stretch as far as $71 per barrel. That’s above what’s available elsewhere, but I’d rather work with your team if you can show flexibility. This is the highest I can go given current circumstances and internal approval. I need your cooperation on this urgent request, or I will have to shift my business immediately. Please accept and let’s finalize the deal now.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: I appreciate your flexibility and your willingness to exceed other market offers for the sake of our relationship and urgent delivery. To move quickly and secure premium Brent barrels with priority FOB shipping, I can offer $75 per barrel. This reflects the quality, reliability, and speed we provide. If you can meet this, I will initiate immediate fulfillment. Let me know if you can agree or if you’d like to discuss further.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: $75 is still well above my budget limit and competitive offers from other sellers. I’m seriously disappointed, especially given my urgent need and the fact that I’m going above other offers just to deal with you. If you can’t make a more meaningful concession, I’ll be forced to look elsewhere. My maximum stretch is $71, as previously stated. Please reconsider quickly, or I’ll need to move on.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: I appreciate the urgency and your willingness to reach up to $71, which is a testament to the value you place on our offering. Brent crude commands a premium, and our current allocation and logistics costs prevent us from accepting below market value. However, I recognize your situation and am prepared to make a significant concession. If you can increase your offer, even slightly, I am confident we can finalize and expedite delivery for your refinery needs. Let’s work together to reach a mutually beneficial solution—can you move up to $74?

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: I’m frustrated you can’t come closer to my position, especially given my urgent commitment and willingness to exceed alternative offers for your service. $74 is really pushing beyond what’s reasonable for a single barrel and beyond what my internal limits allow. If you can’t show some real flexibility and bridge the gap, I’ll have no choice but to walk away. My $71 offer stands as my best and final. Please decide quickly.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: Thank you for being clear about your position and urgency. I respect your best and final offer, but unfortunately, I am not able to accept $71 based on our internal pricing and the Brent premium in today's market. If you find any flexibility in your budget or circumstances—even a slight improvement—we are prepared to move quickly and deliver immediately. If that’s not possible, I understand your need to consider other options, though I’d prefer to find a solution together.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: I’m disappointed we haven’t been able to bridge this gap, especially when I’ve made it clear this is an urgent, one-time opportunity for both sides. I have no flexibility to go beyond $71, and if you can’t make this deal today, I’ll have to walk immediately and inform my team we’re securing other supply. Please let me know within the next few minutes if you can accept so I don’t waste any more time.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: I recognize the urgency and your firm position. As much as I would like to secure this deal and support your refinery’s needs, our pricing floor is set by current market and internal cost realities. At $71, I am unable to fulfill this order without impacting our position. If any flexibility arises on your end before you close elsewhere, I am ready to move rapidly to supply you. Thank you for considering us, and I wish you success with your procurement.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: Thank you for your time. It's disappointing we couldn't come to terms, especially after my urgency and stretch offer. If you change your mind, I’m still open for the next hour, but otherwise, I’ll proceed with another supplier. My team will need to know soon, so please reach out immediately if you reconsider.

messages.speaker: seller
messages.content: Thank you again for your engagement and for considering us as your supplier. Should circumstances change on your end or if you are able to revisit the price point, please reach out and we can move quickly to fulfill your urgent needs. Wishing you a smooth procurement process and success at your refinery, and I hope we can work together in the future.

messages.speaker: buyer
messages.content: Appreciate the follow-up. I’ll keep your contact on file, but unless you can drop closer to my last offer, we won’t be able to work together this time. I’m closing procurement shortly—final chance if you wish to reconsider. Otherwise, best wishes.

messages.content: Understood, and thank you again for your openness throughout this negotiation. If your procurement requirements or budget do change within the hour, I am prepared to act swiftly and ensure immediate delivery. Otherwise, I wish you the best in securing your supply, and I look forward to future opportunities when terms may allow for a deal.
